title: Pensions and insurance clerks and assistants
description: Examine, evaluate, and investigate eligibility for or conformity with laws and regulations governing contract compliance of licenses and permits, and perform other compliance and enforcement inspection and analysis activities not classified elsewhere. Inspect and investigate sources of pollution to protect the public and environment and ensure conformance with Federal, State, and local regulations and ordinances. Monitor and evaluate compliance with equal opportunity laws, guidelines, and policies to ensure that employment practices and contracting arrangements give equal opportunity without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, age, or disability. (Based on 17 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Analyze monthly department budgeting and accounting reports to maintain expenditure controls.

Prepare and process import and export documentation according to customs regulations, laws, or procedures, and communicate with regulatory agencies regarding regulatory compliance and documentation needs.
Coordinate, prepare, or review regulatory submissions for domestic or international projects, ensuring compliance with regulations and policies.
Compile and maintain regulatory documentation databases or systems, and stay abreast of changes in import or export laws or regulations.
Review product promotional materials, labeling, and records for compliance with regulations, and prepare correspondence or reports related to inspections or investigations.
Develop or conduct employee regulatory training and assist project teams with regulatory requirements and compliance issues.
Monitor investigations of suspected violations to ensure compliance with regulations and assist in audits or inspection follow-ups.
Collect and analyze data to detect deficiencies or fraud, and recommend changes to ensure compliance with laws and regulations.
Review clinical protocols and applications for compliance with regulatory requirements and eligibility issues.
Inspect government property to ensure compliance with contract specifications, licensing, or regulatory requirements.
Prepare, examine, or analyze financial records, reports, or statements to ensure accuracy and compliance with standards, and report findings to management.
Certify eligibility for benefits, provide information on application details, and assist with claims processes.
Manage communication with stakeholders regarding licensing, permits, and other regulatory matters, and prepare detailed reports of evaluations and recommendations.
Review equal opportunity complaints, conduct interviews, and develop guidelines for nondiscriminatory practices.
Inspect product safety and compliance with handling procedures, and coordinate recall or market withdrawal activities, if necessary.
Interview clients to obtain information needed for preparing simple to complex tax returns and gather additional information on taxable income and deductible expenses.
Review and verify data on insurance applications and policies, such as age, name, address, coverage amounts, and determine coverage based on insurance policy.
Determine nature of code violations, maintain and prepare inspection records, and monitor compliance with regulations.
Investigate complaints, interview individuals to determine suspected violations, and prepare reports summarizing findings regarding illegal dumping, pollution, and related laws.
Provide customer service, answer inquiries regarding claims, and explain available services or procedures for various financial matters including insurance claims and loans.
Compile and analyze accounting records or budget estimates to determine financial resources required and ensure conformance to procedures and regulations.
Use appropriate deductions and credits to keep clients' taxes to a minimum while ensuring compliance with applicable tax laws and regulations.

Monitor compliance with equal opportunity laws and regulations, investigate employment practices for discriminatory factors, and provide technical assistance and training on employee supervision and hiring procedures.
Process new insurance policies and claims, review documentation for accuracy, and transmit claims for payment or further investigation as necessary.
Prepare and maintain records of loans and credit applications, ensuring accuracy and compliance with regulatory standards.

Examine permits, licenses, applications, and records to ensure compliance with licensing requirements, provide relevant information to the public regarding regulations, and prepare related documentation.
Perform audits of financial records and transactions, ensuring adherence to established guidelines and preparing reports for audit findings.
Conduct surveys to gather data regarding civil rights complaints, and coordinate procedures to ensure timely review and correction of discriminatory factors based on findings.
Organize or work with detailed office or warehouse records, maintaining files for each policyholder, including policies that are to be reinstated or cancelled.
